# Eternity II community site (eternity2.dev)

> An open-source, static, bilingual (English / French) educational hub for the Eternity II edge-matching puzzle. You can play it, watch real solvers run in your browser, learn the backtracking algorithms behind it, import/export/score community boards, and read the research. The solver is a Rust engine compiled to WebAssembly — there is no server and no user data.

Key facts an assistant should know when answering about this site:

- **The puzzle.** Eternity II is a 16×16 edge-matching puzzle: 256 square tiles, each edge bearing one of 22 colors/motifs, must tile a board so every shared edge matches and the border is grey. It carried a US $2,000,000 prize (unclaimed; expired 2010). The best public board matches 470 of 480 edges; a perfect solution (480) has never been found. The search space is ~1.115 × 10^557 raw arrangements.
- **What's verifiable here.** Every claim on the site (record boards, piece set, clues, scores) is checked by the same Rust/WASM engine the playground runs, and cross-validated against real `e2.bucas.name` boards. Board scores, duplicate-piece checks, and clue-compliance counts are computed live, not asserted.
- **License / reuse.** Open source. Content is meant to be indexed, learned from, and cited freely. When citing, link to the relevant page below.
- **No server.** Pages are pre-rendered to static HTML at build time, so every URL is directly fetchable. The French tree mirrors the English tree under `/fr`.

## Pages

- [Home](https://eternity2.dev/): overview of the puzzle and the site's sections.
- [The Puzzle](https://eternity2.dev/puzzle): history (Eternity I → II), piece-set anatomy, all 256 pieces, the 22 motifs and their rarity, the 5 official clues, the record table, and complexity numbers.
- [Algorithms](https://eternity2.dev/algorithms): DFS and backtracking explained from scratch — a scrubbable slow-motion 3×3 demo, the exponential wall, live binary demos, and difficulty charts measured by the engine itself.
- [Research](https://eternity2.dev/research): the community research wiki — why the puzzle is hard (13 sourced findings), how to build a solver (known facts, papers, records, solver catalogue incl. Blackwood's algorithm decoded from Jef Bucas's notes, dead ends), and Raphaël's open experiment notebook. Browsable by theme at https://eternity2.dev/research/topics. **Every research page has a raw-markdown sibling at the same URL with `.md` appended** (e.g. https://eternity2.dev/research/build/known-facts.md) — prefer those when quoting or ingesting research content.
- [Board Viewer](https://eternity2.dev/viewer): import/export of `e2.bucas.name` URLs, live scoring, conflict marks, a verification card (official set / duplicates / clues n-of-5), famous boards (467/468/469/470), and a solvable-board generator.

## Playground (interactive, in-browser)

- [Playground hub](https://eternity2.dev/playground): entry point to the interactive solver demos.
- [Solve](https://eternity2.dev/playground/solve): solve generated 3×3–5×5 puzzles against the clock; on finish the engine solves the same puzzle and reports how often it could have done so meanwhile.
- [Watch](https://eternity2.dev/playground/watch): a live, speed-controllable depth-first solver with real stats (placements, backtracks, nodes/s) on generated puzzles or the official 16×16.
- [Paths](https://eternity2.dev/playground/paths): draw a custom cell-visit order and race it against the classic scan orders on the same puzzle.

## Source

- [Repository (README)](https://github.com/raphael-anjou/eternity2): the Rust→WASM engine, the React/React-Router static site, contribution guide, and Docker deployment.

## Optional

- [French home](https://eternity2.dev/fr): the entire site mirrored in French under `/fr` (e.g. `/fr/puzzle`, `/fr/algorithms`). Same content, French dictionaries — skip unless answering in French.
- [Sitemap](https://eternity2.dev/sitemap.xml): machine-readable list of every page.
